subject: Configure APPC ODL cluster for persistent storage
commit_message:
Configure APPC ODL cluster for persistent storage

Change-Id: I1ceb0ee27bd758f9d1dac5e3dfac808dbeaae6ab
Issue-ID: OOM-779
Signed-off-by: BorislavG <Borislav.Glozman@amdocs.com>

Patch Set 1:

> (1 comment)

There was a decision to make the directory under /dockerdata-nfs to be "Release.Name", which is the name of the helm deployed release and not the namespace. It will provide the ability to deploy on the same environment in the same namespace a "test" helm release and after testing "prod" helm release.

Patch Set 1:

I've tried this out and one thing I've noticed is that these new PVs are created under  /<mountpath>/<release name>, whereas most apps are putting their data under /<mountpath>/<namespace name>.  I'm not sure which is right, my take is that using release name is better, but at least for now we're not consistent.

Patch Set 2:

James, See this: https://jira.onap.org/browse/OOM-907

Release.Name is where we want to go.  The consistency will be address by 907.

Patch Set 2:

Can we merge this?

(1 comment)

I will be porting this to SDN-C shortly

Patch Set 2: Code-Review-1

> (1 comment)
 > 
 > Can this be merged?

I verified this morning that the issue I reported previously is still present. A view after the install and after the uninstall are shown below. The volumes never get cleaned up properly.


 > (1 comment)
 > 
 > Can this be merged?

The issue raised previously is still present as verified this morning. A view after install and uninstall is below. The volumes never get cleaned up in my environment.

Patch Set 2:

@Aaron Hay  I think what you're seeing is intended behaviour.  We want the PVs to survive the teardown of the pods so that the data is still available.  You should be able to re-deploy and any files in these PVs will still be there and available to be imported.

Patch Set 2:

> @Aaron Hay  I think what you're seeing is intended behaviour.  We
 > want the PVs to survive the teardown of the pods so that the data
 > is still available.  You should be able to re-deploy and any files
 > in these PVs will still be there and available to be imported.

Can the steps be provided for how to do that so I can test that it works? I'm not able to reinstall with the same configuration without those steps.

Patch Set 2: Code-Review+1

The issue in my environment is not seen in other similar environments.
Patch Set 3: Patch Set 2 was rebased
Patch Set 3: Code-Review+2 Verified+1
Change has been successfully merged by Mike Elliott

Patch Set 3:

FYI after this was merged, I'm getting this error on helm install:

Error: error validating "": error validating data: found invalid field imagePullSecrets for v1.PersistentVolumeClaimSpec

I've updated OOM-779 to report this issue.

Patch Set 3:
